steps.title: Recognize the focus of the passage
steps.content: The passage centers on what Lutie perceives at a specific moment. It opens with the visual quality of the street at sunset, records her thought (“the street is nice in this light”), and then catalogs the activities she observes—children playing, skipping rope, and other games. The entire passage is structured as observation and description.

steps.title: Match the focus to the choices
steps.content: Choice A directly captures this: Lutie observing the street's appearance at a particular time of day and the events occurring on it. Choices B, C, and D all contradict the passage—introducing annoyance, confusion, or avoidance that simply aren't present in the text. That matches choice A — A is correct.
